user_query:
Please help me prioritize our next localized onboarding rollout from a global perspective.

1) Download two official datasets from The World Bank Open Data portal and save them locally for reproducibility:
   - Latest available CSV for the indicator "Total population (SP.POP.TOTL)" for all countries -> save to downloads/population.csv
   - Latest available CSV for the indicator "Individuals using the Internet (% of population) (IT.NET.USER.ZS)" for all countries -> save to downloads/internet_users_percent.csv

2) Using only the candidate countries in input/markets.csv (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes), compute for each:
   - internet_users_estimate = population * internet_users_percent / 100
   - ux_reach_score = 0.7 * internet_users_estimate + 0.3 * population

3) Rank countries by ux_reach_score (descending) and classify into tiers:
   - Tier 1: top 20% of candidates
   - Tier 2: next 30%
   - Tier 3: remaining 50%

4) Produce these outputs:
   - outputs/market_priorities.csv with columns: country_code, population, internet_users_percent, internet_users_estimate, ux_reach_score, tier
   - outputs/summary.json containing: { "data_sources": [{"source": "The World Bank Open Data", "indicator_id": "SP.POP.TOTL", "title": "Total population", "access_date": "YYYY-MM-DD"}, {"source": "The World Bank Open Data", "indicator_id": "IT.NET.USER.ZS", "title": "Individuals using the Internet (% of population)", "access_date": "YYYY-MM-DD"}], "candidates_count": <number processed>, "missing_count": <number skipped> }
   - outputs/missing_data.csv listing any candidate country_code skipped and the reason (e.g., missing population or internet_users_percent)

5) Edit the existing document input/localization_plan.md by rewriting the section titled "Localization priorities":
   - Replace its current content with a bullet list of the top 10 countries (or fewer if fewer are available) showing country_code and the rounded ux_reach_score.
   - Add a single-sentence justification that references this data-driven approach and our need to coordinate across time zones.
   - Preserve all other sections and headings as-is.

Constraints:
- Use only the candidate country codes in input/markets.csv; do not add others.
- Use the most recent available year in the downloaded datasets for each indicator.
- Do not hardcode any values; compute everything from the downloaded CSVs and save those CSVs under downloads/.
- If multiple years are present, pick the latest year with non-null values for each country.
- Be deterministic: ensure outputs can be recomputed from the saved downloads and input files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
